creator: simon@comsys.ntu-kpi.kiev.ua
text:
On Sun, 7 Jan 2001, Andreas Klemm wrote:

> The following reply was made to PR ports/24110; it has been noted by GNATS.
> 
> From: Andreas Klemm <andreas@FreeBSD.org>
> To: Peter Pentchev <roam@orbitel.bg>
> Cc: andreas@FreeBSD.org, FreeBSD-gnats-submit@FreeBSD.org
> Subject: Re: ports/24110: ipa port (sysutils/ipa) doesn't honor CFLAGS
> Date: Sun, 7 Jan 2001 22:07:15 +0100
> 
>  On Sun, Jan 07, 2001 at 08:44:18PM +0200, Peter Pentchev wrote:
>  > On Sat, Jan 06, 2001 at 05:06:33PM +0100, andreas@FreeBSD.org wrote:
>  > [snip]
>  > > --- Makefile.orig	Sat Jan  6 16:48:50 2001
>  > > +++ Makefile	Sat Jan  6 16:50:22 2001
>  > > @@ -38,7 +38,7 @@
>  > >  #	-O3   - optimize (it seems that -O3 also help to find out some errors);
>  > >  #	-g    - produce debugging information.
>  > >  #
>  > > -CFLAGS=-Wall -O3 -g
>  > > +#CFLAGS=-Wall -O3 -g
>  > 
>  > Wouldn't this be better off with something like:
>  > 
>  > CFLAGS ?= -Wall -O3 -g
>  > 
>  > That's the way it's done in other ports; that way, it allows the user
>  > to manually build/test software from within ${WRKSRC} without having
>  > to make CFLAGS='...' each time.
>  
>  Good idea.
>  

As I understood, I should add `CFLAGS?=...' to Makefile of my software
(not to Makefile of port). But on my FreeBSD 4.1-STABLE ${CFLAGS} variable
is already predefined to "-O -pipe", I think that the same results will
be on some other systems.

So, this method doesn't fix problem with value of CFLAGS variable.
